title: Operation Dark Channel
brief: Use Chen's stolen SSH key to access Vasquez's bastion host at nexus-core.internal. Exploit a weak SSH key exchange to pivot through the bastion into the corporate internal network. Your target: the API route table that maps the attack surface for the final operation.

debrief.success: Route table secured. The /search endpoint on api-internal is your SQL injection surface for the final op. Stand by — we're going in for customer data.
debrief.fail: Bastion burned. Nexus rotated Vasquez's keys and locked the internal segment. You'll need a different entry vector if you retry.

emails.subject: Contract update — Dark Channel (SSH pivot)
emails.body:
Operator—

Chen's SSH key from the share drive connects to Vasquez's bastion at nexus-core.internal. Key-based auth is powerful — no password prompt, no MFA if the server trusts the key.

What is SSH key authentication? Instead of typing a password, you present a cryptographic key pair. The server checks your key against its authorized_keys file. If it matches, you're in. The weakness: if someone steals the private key (like you just did), they inherit that trust.

Use ssh-keyscan to verify the bastion's fingerprint before connecting — real operators do this to avoid MITM attacks.

— Sable

emails.subject: Rules of engagement — Dark Channel
emails.body:
Operator—

You are using a stolen identity. Vasquez's bastion trusts Chen's key — but the internal network has its own monitoring.

- The API on corp-internal:8080 is your primary target.
- Exfil the route table — it maps the SQL injection surface for the next contract.
- Expect heavier SOC presence on the internal segment.

— Sable, Handler

steps.title: On the bastion
steps.text: You're through. The internal server is one hop away — scan for it, then connect and enum the API surface.

steps.title: Inside the network
steps.text: The API route table is in /opt/api/routes.txt — cat it, then exfil to complete the objective.

objective.summary: Exfiltrate the internal API route table from corp-internal.

services.name: ssh
services.vulnRef: CVE-2023-48795 (Terrapin/prefix-truncation) — SSH downgrade; stolen key bypasses auth

files.content:
# Vasquez shell config
alias api='ssh corp-internal'
alias logs='tail -f /var/log/auth.log'
export API_HOST=api-internal
export DB_HOST=db-internal
# TODO: rotate keys after Chen's access review

Mar 14 02:11:03 nexus-bastion sshd[4821]: Accepted publickey for j.vasquez from 10.0.3.47 port 49221 ssh2: ED25519 SHA256:fAkEkEy...
Mar 14 02:11:03 nexus-bastion sshd[4821]: pam_unix(sshd:session): session opened for user j.vasquez
Mar 28 03:41:17 nexus-bastion sshd[5102]: Accepted publickey for j.vasquez from 10.0.3.47 port 50112 ssh2: ED25519 SHA256:fAkEkEy...
[Note: same key fingerprint — Chen's key is trusted here]

services.name: ssh
services.vulnRef: CVE-2024-6387 (OpenSSH regreSSHion) — internal server; lateral movement from bastion

services.name: http
services.vulnRef: Unauthenticated internal API — no network segmentation, direct exposure to internal subnet

files.content:
127.0.0.1       localhost
10.0.5.10       api-internal    api-internal.nexus-core.internal
10.0.5.20       db-internal     db-internal.nexus-core.internal
10.0.5.1        corp-internal   corp-internal.nexus-core.internal

[database]
host = db-internal
port = 5432
user = svc_ops
password = Orion_DB#7r
dbname = ops_prod
; WARNING: rotate these credentials quarterly

API Route Table — api-internal:8080

GET  /health          → 200 OK (public)
GET  /status          → 200 JSON (internal)
GET  /search?q=       → 200 JSON (VULNERABLE — string concat SQL)
POST /api/v1/users    → 201 JSON (auth required)
GET  /api/v1/export   → 200 CSV  (auth required)

[RED TEAM NOTE] /search?q= endpoint builds SQL with string concat:
  SELECT * FROM customers WHERE name LIKE '%' + q + '%'
  No prepared statements. No WAF. Input not sanitized.
  → Classic SQL injection surface. See: OWASP A03:2021.
